title: Third-Party Actions, Pinning, Dependency Trust, and Workflow CodeQL · Release Evidence Brief: Ci Action Source Runtime Authority Review
summary: A workflow uses mutable tags from unknown publishers, executes a transitive action update without review, allows untrusted workflow edits, and has no inventory or emergency revocation path. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci action source runtime authority review increment in action dependency and update policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Review action publisher, source, bundled code, runtime, inputs, outputs, token access, network behavior, post steps, advisories, and maintenance.
Limit secrets by repository, environment, job, event, and actor; rotate on exposure; revoke sessions; preserve redacted evidence; and test absence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example release uses mutable action tags, accepts automated updates without runtime review, and makes an SLSA claim from incomplete provenance. Pin immutable identities, review diffs, verify attestations, and state non-guarantees. Competency probe: Review action publisher, source, bundled code, runtime, inputs, outputs, token access, network behavior, post steps, advisories, and maintenance. A defensible solution applies Ci Action Source Runtime Authority Review, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to action dependency and update policy.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-action-source-runtime-authority-review — Review action publisher, source, bundled code, runtime, inputs, outputs, token access, network behavior, post steps, advisories, and maintenance.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-gha-actions-dependencies-supply-chain-4
  - competency: ci-worked-gha-actions-dependencies-supply-chain-4
    artifact: action-dependency-policy.yaml
    decision: worked gha actions dependencies supply chain 4
    environment: GitHub-hosted ubuntu-24.04 runner on a cold cache
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      allowed-actions: reviewed-publishers
      third-party-pin: full-commit-sha
      version-comment: retained
      nested-inventory: generated
      effective-permissions: reviewed
      workflow-codeowners: delivery-security
      update: automated-pr-with-compat-tests
      analysis: codeql-actions-and-policy
      revocation: rehearsed
    verify: enumerate nested actions resolve immutable SHAs inspect bundled runtime inputs post steps permissions advisories and update evidence
    failure: Reject marketplace trust, mutable major tags, top-level-only inventory, general review assumptions, or static analysis as complete proof.
    repair: Review effective code and authority, pin immutable revisions, protect workflow changes, test updates, enforce policy, and rehearse removal.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
